competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty analysis, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and various systems of units including SI units (fundamental and derived).
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to check consistency of physical equations and derive relationships between quantities.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, understanding different types of motion, and interpreting graphical representations.
competencies: Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration for describing motion.
Distinguish between uniform and non-uniform motion and calculate average speed/velocity versus instantaneous velocity.
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using both graphical methods and calculus.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector algebra, resolution of vectors, and analyzing two-dimensional motion including projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Perform operations on scalar and vector quantities (addition, subtraction, multiplication) and resolve vectors into rectangular components.
Analyze uniform velocity/acceleration in a plane, including projectile motion and uniform circular motion.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Dynamics and Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, momentum conservation, friction, and dynamics of circular motion.
competencies: Apply Newton's three laws of motion including the concept of inertia and impulse.
Use the law of conservation of linear momentum in various applications.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Principles
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work-energy theorem, potential energy concepts, conservative forces, and collision analysis.
competencies: Apply the work-energy theorem and calculate power for constant and variable forces.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze motion in a vertical circle using energy conservation.
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions (1D and 2D) based on momentum and energy principles.

competency_groups.title: System of Particles - Rotational Motion Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass, torque, angular momentum conservation, rotational kinematics/dynamics, and moment of inertia.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod).
Apply laws of conservation of linear and angular momentum to rotating systems.
Analyze equilibrium of rigid bodies and compare linear motion with rotational motion using torque, moment of inertia, and radius of gyration.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Universal Law and Orbital Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Kepler's laws, gravitational fields/potentials, variation of gravity, and satellite motion.
competencies: Apply Kepler's laws and the universal law of gravitation to planetary motion.
Analyze variations in acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate escape speed, orbital velocity, and energy associated with orbiting satellites.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids and Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to elasticity, stress-strain relationships, fluid properties (viscosity, surface tension), and Bernoulli's theorem.
competencies: Apply Hooke's law and calculate elastic moduli (Young’s, Bulk, Shear) and Poisson's ratio for solids.
Analyze fluid statics including pressure variation, Pascal's law, and hydrostatic forces.
Explain phenomena related to viscosity, terminal velocity, streamline flow, Bernoulli's theorem, surface tension, and capillary action.

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties - Heat and Temperature
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal expansion, specific heat, calorimetry, phase changes, and modes of heat transfer.
competencies: Explain anomalous expansion of water and calculate effects of thermal expansion in solids, liquids, and gases.
Apply concepts of specific heat capacity (Cp, Cv) and latent heat to solve calorimetric problems.
Analyze modes of heat transfer: conduction, convection, radiation, and apply Stefan's law/Blackbody radiation principles.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal equilibrium, laws of thermodynamics, state variables, and gaseous processes.
competencies: Apply the Zeroth law for defining temperature and distinguish between heat, work, and internal energy.
Use the First Law (conservation of energy) and Second Law concepts regarding entropy and efficiency in cyclic processes.
Analyze isothermal, adiabatic, reversible/irreversible, and cyclic gaseous processes.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory - Gas Behavior
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to kinetic theory assumptions, molecular speed distributions, degrees of freedom, and specific heats.
competencies: Derive the equation of state for a perfect gas from kinetic theory assumptions regarding pressure and temperature.
Calculate RMS speed and apply the law of equipartition of energy to determine specific heat capacities of gases.
Understand concepts like mean free path and Avogadro's number in the context of molecular behavior.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and simple pendulums.
competencies: Define periodic functions and describe displacement as a function of time for S.H.M.
Derive the expression for the time period of a simple pendulum and analyze uniform circular motion relations to S.H.M.

competency_groups.title: Waves - Wave Motion and Superposition
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, and interference.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe the speed of traveling waves.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Charges, Fields, and Flux
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields, dipoles, Gauss's theorem applications, and potential energy.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's law and the superposition principle for systems of charges and continuous distributions.
Calculate electric field due to point charges, dipoles, and apply Gauss's theorem to symmetric charge configurations (wire, sheet, shell).
Analyze torque on an electric dipole in a uniform field.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential - Capacitance Concepts
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electric potential, equipotential surfaces, dielectrics, capacitors, and energy storage.
competencies: Calculate electric potential due to point charges, dipoles, and systems of charges; identify equipotential surfaces.
Analyze the behavior of conductors/insulators, polarization in dielectrics, and capacitance variations with geometry/dielectric medium.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Ohm's Law and Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to drift velocity, resistivity, temperature dependence of resistance, cells, Kirchhoff's laws, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate electric current to drift velocity and mobility; apply Ohm's law for linear/non-linear conductors.

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Fields, Forces, and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to magnetic fields (Biot-Savart/Ampere), forces on charges/conductors, torque on loops, and galvanometer applications.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart law and Ampere's law to calculate magnetic fields for various current configurations.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Matter Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to magnetic dipoles, properties of materials (para/dia/ferro), and temperature effects.
competencies: Analyze the behavior of bar magnets as equivalent solenoids and calculate torque on magnetic dipoles.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Laws and Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction.
competencies: Apply Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction and Lenz's law to determine induced EMF.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current - AC Circuits and Power
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to RMS values, reactance/impedance, LCR circuits, resonance, power factor, generators, and transformers.
competencies: Analyze series LCR circuits using phasors including concepts of resonance.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Nature
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to displacement current, transverse nature of EM waves, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Understand the concept of displacement current and the characteristics/transverse nature of electromagnetic waves.

competency_groups.title: Optics - Ray Optics and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reflection, refraction, mirrors, lenses, prisms, optical instruments (microscopes/telescopes).
competencies: Apply mirror formula, lens maker's formula, and thin lens combinations.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics - Interference, Diffraction, Polarization
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Huygen's principle, interference (Young's experiment), diffraction.
competencies: Apply Huygen's principle to derive laws of reflection and refraction.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to photoelectric effect, Einstein's equation, de-Broglie relation.
competencies: Explain the particle nature of light through the photoelectric effect using Einstein's equation.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms - Models and Spectra
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Rutherford's model, Bohr's postulates, hydrogen spectra.
competencies: Describe the structure of the atom based on Rutherford scattering and apply Bohr's model for energy levels.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei - Structure, Binding Energy, Fission/Fusion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to nuclear composition, mass defect, binding energy curve, fission and fusion.
competencies: Analyze the variation of binding energy per nucleon with mass number.

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy bands, intrinsic/extrinsic semiconductors, p-n junctions, rectification.
competencies: Distinguish between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory.
